classification.gcvs.reason: GCVS and NSV rows, cross-identifiers, variable classes, periods, extrema, spectral strings, and bibliography remain complete inspectable evidence. Only 19 current canonical stars resolve uniquely through the release-native cross-identifier bridge, so E5 must not manufacture a broad name or coordinate crossmatch; later release-scoped identity work may promote uniquely reviewed bindings.

clusters.hunt_reffert_2024.reason: All 465 bounded Hunt/Reffert clusters receive an exact source-designation outcome with an inverse collision safeguard: 62 bind one-to-one, 393 are absent from the current canonical reference, and 10 remain ambiguous. Coherent cluster posteriors are eligible only on accepted clusters. All 51,017 member claims preserve their published probabilities with independently resolved Gaia DR3 endpoints; none creates canonical containment.

compact.atnf.reason: The reproducible compact-scope audit finds no distinct safe canonical pulsar leaf. Its only exact SIMBAD/Gaia route, PSR J0437-4715, reaches a lone K-spectrum Gaia leaf with a legacy pulsar type and is quarantined as a pulsar/optical-companion scope conflict. Preserve every ATNF parameter, glitch, reference, and alias until E6 creates or binds distinct compact-object identity without coordinate guessing or companion leakage.

compact.mcgill_magnetar.reason: The reproducible compact-scope audit finds no McGill name bound to a distinct current canonical magnetar leaf. Preserve all coherent timing, X-ray, distance, position, association, activity, and bibliography contexts until E6 adds or binds compact-object identity inside the ingestion envelope.

exoplanet_lifecycle.exoplanet_eu.reason: All 8,261 source planets receive exact normalized-name outcomes against the NASA-rooted canonical reference: 4,715 bind and 3,546 remain missing. All 8,261 positive lifecycle assertions and 46,819 parameter facts remain conflict-preserving supplemental evidence; none creates inventory or overrides NASA authority.

exoplanet_lifecycle.hwc.reason: All 5,599 HWC rows receive exact normalized-name outcomes: 5,568 bind and 31 remain missing. All 161,706 facts remain explicitly derived habitability-comparison evidence and are never treated as direct measurement authority or lifecycle confirmation.

exoplanet_lifecycle.open_exoplanet_catal.reason: All 9,253 structural planet objects resolve through their complete source-native name sets: 5,095 bind, 4,154 remain missing, and four alias conflicts remain ambiguous. All lifecycle polarities, 9,252 coherent parameter sets, 51,706 facts, and the HD 99492 c positive/negative conflict remain inspectable without canonical status mutation.

extended.green_snr.reason: All 310 Green SNR rows bind through exact canonical source-reconciliation keys. Source geometry, morphology, flux, spectral index, names, citations, and lineage remain extended-object evidence; no row enters stellar facts.

extended.openngc_and_nebulae.reason: All 19,012 OpenNGC and constituent-catalog evidence rows receive an exact canonical source-reconciliation outcome: 17,800 bind, 803 are explicit exclusions, 404 remain quarantined, and five redirects lack targets. Geometry, distance, parameters, components, and citations remain in the extended-object domain and never become stellar facts.

gaia.dr3.external_crossmatches.reason: Official external-crossmatch rows are identity/scope evidence and are not independently selectable scientific quantities.

gaia.dr3.non_single_star.reason: All 87,075 coherent Gaia NSS source/model solutions receive an exact Gaia DR3 observation-target outcome. The 56,617 solutions on 54,794 current canonical sources remain complete context with model, fitted parameters/errors, correlations, diagnostics, frame, and reference; 30,458 missing-reference outcomes remain explicit. Because NSS exposes no inspectable component endpoints, every solution requires relation adjudication and none creates a companion, containment edge, relation, selected scalar, or simulation-ready orbit.

multiplicity.debcat.reason: All 374 DEBCat systems receive a priority-aware exact-name outcome. Component physics becomes eligible only through a unique canonical system, exact WDS anchor, and unique period-compatible accepted MSC relation; integrated photometry and metallicity remain system scoped. The projection creates no canonical star or containment edge.

multiplicity.el_badry_2021_wide_binary.reason: E5 independently binds both release-scoped Gaia EDR3 endpoints through the official EDR3/DR3 source-list identity, accounts every endpoint outcome, preserves candidates and shifted-sky controls, and marks the source-defined R_chance_align < 0.1 subset as high-confidence relation evidence without manufacturing a strict probability, hierarchy edge, or canonical containment.

multiplicity.msc.reason: Every release-native MSC component identity, parameter set, classification, photometry row, astrometry row, hierarchy relation, and orbit solution receives an explicit scope outcome. Selectable facts require an accepted WDS-scoped component or exact accepted relation; relative separation remains context-only evidence, and no source component becomes a canonical star or containment edge.

multiplicity.orb6.reason: Every ORB6 visual-orbit row receives an exact WDS-pair and MSC-endpoint outcome. Published solutions become eligible only when the punctuation-preserving WDS identifier plus combined discoverer/pair designation resolves to one WDS row and the documented component notation resolves to one accepted MSC relation. No system-level orbit fallback or containment is created.

multiplicity.sb9.reason: Every SB9 relation receives an exact MSC sequence-reference outcome. Component magnitudes, classifications, and spectroscopic orbit solutions become eligible only when exactly one MSC relation carries the sequence and both release-scoped endpoints resolve; missing, duplicate, and unresolved references remain explicit.

multiplicity.sbx.reason: Every SBX spectroscopic system receives an exact authoritative-identifier consensus outcome, including the official Gaia DR2-to-DR3 route. Primary and secondary components remain release scoped beneath accepted system anchors; their classifications, magnitudes, and orbit solutions become eligible without assuming the catalog observation target is the primary. Astrometry remains system context, source hierarchy claims remain noncanonical relation evidence, and no component or containment edge is promoted into CORE.

multiplicity.wds.reason: All 157,299 WDS visual-pair summaries receive a documented component-parser and exact MSC endpoint outcome. The 5,282 uniquely resolved pairs retain scoped spectral, component-magnitude, relative-astrometry, epoch, position, and source-convention proper-motion context. Opaque spectral text is never split onto stars, unspecified-band magnitudes and source-unit astrometry are not selectable facts, the CDS positional bridge is not treated as identity, and no canonical component or containment edge is created.

multiplicity.wds_gaia_xmatch.reason: WDS-to-Gaia rows are component endpoint crossmatch evidence, not standalone scientific quantities.

solar_system.jpl_horizons_artificial.reason: Artificial-spacecraft controls verify source and orbit contracts but are excluded from the public natural-object selection projection.

solar_system.jpl_horizons_authority.reason: All 60 reviewed natural Horizons targets bind through exact source keys or canonical JPL-command metadata. Fifty-nine target/physical-center pairs are epoch/frame-bound orbit-selection inputs; the Sun-to-barycenter solution remains declared reference-origin context. Every solution preserves the complete 12-element source row. All 36 coherent physical sets remain bound to exact natural objects, and no relation is promoted canonically.

tess.identity_and_candidate_evidence.reason: All 27,930 targeted TICs receive official Gaia DR2-to-DR3 graph outcomes and all 8,064 TOIs retain disposition polarity and host status. Exactly 824 confirmed/known TOIs link to existing canonical planets through one accepted host and unique period; 5,383 candidates, 1,346 negative rows, 508 unresolved confirmed rows, and three unclassified rows never enter canonical inventory.

tess.villanova_eb.reason: All 17,605 Villanova rows receive an exact release-scoped TIC observation-target outcome. Positive and negative membership, sectors, morphology, source/flags, Tmag, target-context physics, astrometry, and timing solutions remain scoped context. The catalog exposes no inspectable physical endpoints, so every row requires relation adjudication and none creates a component, containment edge, selected scalar, or simulation-ready orbit.

ultracool.gaia_dr3_sample.reason: This source contains probability-bearing ultracool-sample membership context but no independent stellar parameter or classification measurement; membership remains inspectable evidence and cannot invent a selected spectral class.
